content.cn_text: 汤姆:汤姆林森教授，我和安妮可以问你几个关于反思日志作业的问题吗?只是我们对你想让我们包括和讨论的内容有点困惑。
content.en_text: Tom: Professor Tomlinson, may Annie and I please quickly ask you a few questions about the reflective journal assignment? It's just that we're a bit confused as to what you want us to include and discuss.

content.cn_text: 汤姆林森教授:当然可以，你有什么问题?
content.en_text: Prof. Tomlinson: Yes of course, what are you having trouble with?

content.cn_text: 安妮:嗯，所有的事情。首先，在反思日志中首先应该包括什么?也许是别人的建议?
content.en_text: Annie: Well, everything really. To start with, what should be included first in the reflective journal? Perhaps suggestions from others?

content.cn_text: 汤姆林森教授:不，不，首先你应该包括你为自己设定的学习目标
content.en_text: Prof. Tomlinson: No no, firstly you should include the study goals you set yourself at

content.cn_text: 模块的开头。这部分应该在课程开始时由May教授详细讨论过。你应该可以在她提供给课堂的网上幻灯片上找到她的建议。
content.en_text: the beginning of the module. This section should have been discussed in some detail towards the beginning of the course by Professor May. You should be able to find her suggestions on the slides she has provided the class online.

content.cn_text: 安妮:好的，谢谢你，教授。我还可以麻烦您看一下我的参考书目和脚注吗?我觉得他们错过了什么;我们大多数朋友的参考书目都比较长。
content.en_text: Annie: Okay, thank you, Professor. Could I also trouble you to take a brief look at my bibliography and footnotes? I feel like they're missing something; most of our friends'bibliographies are longer.

content.cn_text: 汤姆林森教授:嗯，看看这个，安妮，我可以看出你使用了广泛的资源，这表明你有效地利用了通信技术。据我所知，你不需要对此做任何更改，尽管你可能需要仔细检查你的引用是否符合哈佛引用风格规定。
content.en_text: Prof. Tomlinson: Well, looking at this Annie, I can see that you have used a wide range of resources, which shows that you have made effective use of communication technology. As far as I can tell, you need not make any changes to this, although you might want to double check that your referencing complies with the Harvard Referencing Style regulations.

content.cn_text: 安妮:哦，我很惊讶你会这么说!谢谢你，现在我可以放心了。汤姆，你说你想问教授关于成就部分的事?
content.en_text: Annie: Oh I'm very surprised you've said that! Thank you, now I can set my mind at ease.Tom, you said you wanted to ask the Professor about the achievements section?

content.cn_text: 汤姆:啊，是的，教授，在作业指导中，我们被要求介绍并详细说明我们过去最大的成就，说明我们在这个过程中学到了哪些技能，以及这些技能如何转移到未来不同的职业中。唯一的问题是我不知道我最大的成就是什么。我只在大学暑假期间在酒店餐厅做过服务员。
content.en_text: Tom: Ah yes, Professor, in the assignment guidelines, we are asked to introduce and elaborate on our biggest achievement in the past, saying which skills we learnt in the process, and how these skills can be transferred to various different future careers. The only problem is that I don't know what my greatest achievement actually is. I've only ever worked as a waiter in a hotel restaurant during the summer holidays from university.

content.cn_text: 汤姆林森教授:如果你在一家酒店餐厅当服务员，你肯定会和其他服务员一起工作，作为团队的一部分。你觉得在当服务员的这段时间里，你培养了什么领导才能吗?
content.en_text: Prof. Tomlinson: If you worked as a waiter in a hotel restaurant, you're bound to have worked with other waiters as part of a team. Would you say that during your time as a waiter, you developed any leadership skills?

content.cn_text: 汤姆:是的，我想我被任命为餐饮部的组长。但这很难说是一项成就。
content.en_text: Tom: Yes, well I suppose I was asked to become the team leader of the food and beverage department. But that's hardly an achievement.

content.cn_text: 汤姆林森教授:你可能不这么认为，但如果你写的是你得到了团队领导的职位，这就能更多地展示你的性格，比如你很有魅力，能在高压环境下工作得很好。
content.en_text: Prof. Tomlinson: You might not think so, but if you write that you were offered the position of the team leader, it shows a lot more about your character, for example that you're charismatic and work well in a high-pressure situation.

content.cn_text: 汤姆:我从没想过要把它写下来，谢谢你!我想我应该多听听别人的意见!安妮，你还有什么问题吗还是你准备好回图书馆了?
content.en_text: Tom: I never would have thought to write that down, thank you! I guess I should start listening to others more often! Annie, do you have any more questions or are you ready to go back to the library?

content.cn_text: 安妮:是的，我想我已经得到了我需要的一切。非常感谢，汤姆林森教授!
content.en_text: Annie: Yeah, I think I've got everything I need. Thank you very much, Professor Tomlinson!

content.cn_text: 汤姆:那真的很有帮助。事实上，我现在开始期待写这篇文章了，这应该是一个非常有用的练习，为我们写简历和申请工作做准备。我不善于识别自己的长处和短处，这令人震惊。汤姆林森教授告诉我，我确实需要开始表现出一些自我意识!
content.en_text: Tom: That was really helpful. I'm actually starting to look forward to writing this now, and it should be a really useful exercise to prepare us for writing CVs and applying for jobs. It's shocking how bad I am at identifying my strengths and weaknesses.  Professor Tomlinson has shown me that I definitely need to start displaying some self-awareness!

content.cn_text: 安妮:是啊，汤姆，你真的很喜欢。你总是那么谦虚!在求职的时候，谦虚是很重要的!
content.en_text: Annie: Yeah, Tom, you really do. You're always so modest! Modesty is great until it comes to applying for jobs!

content.cn_text: 安妮:哦，不!我忘了问教授关于识别通过不同活动获得的技能的部分，你还记得吗?当它问你，例如，写一篇文章是否发展了你的学习技能或你的独立学习等等?
content.en_text: Annie: Oh no! I forgot to ask the professor about the section on identifying the skills gained through different activities, do you remember? When it asks you, for example,whether writing an essay develops your study skills or your independent learning and so on?

content.cn_text: 汤姆:哦，天哪，我们真应该问他这个。我也一直有问题。这似乎是一项毫无意义的任务。你认为答案是什么?
content.en_text: Tom: Oh goodness, we really should have asked him that. I've been having trouble with it too. It just seems like such a pointless task. What do you reckon the answers are?

content.cn_text: 安妮:嗯…我认为写一篇文章可能是一种识别和解决问题的方式，因为你必须在引言中陈述问题，然后解决它。我不太确定要不要参加考试……我认为他们应该发展很多不同的技能。如果我必须选择问题28，我会说考试使你对自己更有信心……你同意吗?
content.en_text: Annie: Hmm... I think writing an essay might be a way of identifying and resolving a problem, because you have to state the problem in the introduction and then solve it. I'm not so sure about taking exams... I thought they were supposed to develop lots of different skill sets. If I really had to choose  , I'd say that taking exams enables you to become more confident in yourself.. .do you agree?

content.cn_text: 汤姆:也许吧，我也不知道。你觉得最后两个怎么样?做课堂笔记和演讲笔记?
content.en_text: Tom: Maybe, I really don't know either. What do you think about the last two? Making class notes and presentation notes?

content.cn_text: 安妮:哦，太难了!我认为做课堂笔记是成为一个更独立的学习者的一种方式，因为你自己决定什么是重要的信息并学习它。这提醒了我，我发现做演讲笔记是一场灾难!教授们说得太快了，而我写得太慢了。
content.en_text: Annie: Oh it's so difficult! I think making class notes has to be a way of becoming a more independent learner because you yourself decide what the important information is and learn it. That reminds me,  I find taking presentation notes is a disaster! The professors speak much too quickly, and I write much too slowly, [fade out]

groups.desc:
Questions 21-26
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

items.title: What should be firstly included in the reflective journal?

options.title: A
options.content: topics he is going to talk about

items.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是B（study aims for the module），因为教授明确指出反思日志中首先应该包含的是学生在模块开始时为自己设定的学习目标。"study goals"和"study aims"是同义词，都指学习目标。教授的回答直接对应了题目中的"firstly included"，并明确否定了其他选项，强调了学习目标的重要性。

items.title: The woman has got

options.title: A
options.content: a lot of friends to help her.

options.title: B
options.content: several books that may be useful.

options.title: C
options.content: sufficient resources showing that she is a good technology user.

items.analysis: 正确答案是C，因为教授明确指出Annie使用了广泛的资源，这显示她有效地利用了通信技术。这直接对应了选项C中"sufficient resources showing that she is a good technology user"的描述。教授的评论表明Annie在使用技术获取和利用资源方面表现出色，这正是题目所询问的内容。

items.title: What was the man's biggest achievement in the past?

options.title: A
options.content: He worked as a waiter in a restaurant.

options.title: B
options.content: He got an offer to lead a team.

options.title: C
options.content: He became the chairman of the Student Union.

items.analysis: 文中Tom最初认为自己没有什么大的成就，只提到在酒店餐厅做过服务生。但是在教授的引导下，他意识到被任命为团队领导是一个重要的成就。这表明Tom具有领导能力和在高压环境下工作的能力，这比单纯做服务生更能体现他的成就。因此，被选为团队领导是Tom的最大成就。

items.title: The man decides to
items.analysis: Tom在与教授的对话后意识到自己需要改变。他说"I guess I should start listening to others more often!"，这直接对应了选项C "listen to others"。这句话表明Tom决定要开始更多地听取他人的意见，这是他从对话中得出的结论和决定。

items.title: What is the man's attitude after the discussion?

options.title: B
options.content: He is looking forward to doing the assignment

items.analysis: 文中Tom说"I'm actually starting to look forward to writing this now"，明确表示他开始期待写这份作业。这句话直接反映了Tom对作业的积极态度。此外，他还认为这是一个"really useful exercise"，进一步强调了他对这项任务的正面看法。因此，B选项"He is looking forward to doing the assignment"最准确地描述了Tom的态度。

items.title: What should be shown in the man's reflective journal?
items.analysis: 文中Tom在与教授讨论完反思日志的内容后，总结道他需要开始展现自我意识。这表明自我意识是反思日志中应该展示的关键内容。虽然对话中涉及了其他方面，如成就和技能，但最终Tom意识到自我意识的重要性才是这次讨论的核心。因此，自我意识（self-awareness）是反思日志中应该展示的最重要内容。

groups.desc:
Questions 27-30
What is the woman's attitude toward each of the following activities of study?
Choose FOUR answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-E, next to questions 27-30

items.title: writing an essay
items.analysis: 文中Annie说"writing an essay might be a way of identifying and resolving a problem"，这直接对应了选项A "define a problem"。她进一步解释说在论文中需要在引言部分陈述问题，然后解决它，这个过程实际上就是定义和解决问题的过程。因此，A选项是最符合原文意思的答案。

items.title: taking exams
items.analysis: 文中Annie在讨论不同学习活动时，提到了参加考试。她表示如果必须选择的话，她认为参加考试能让人对自己更有信心。这直接对应了选项D "gain confidence"。虽然Annie的语气有些不确定，但她最终还是选择了这个观点，因此D是最符合原文的答案。

items.title: making class notes
items.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是B (independent learning)，因为原文中Annie明确表示做课堂笔记是成为"more independent learner"（更加独立的学习者）的一种方式。她解释说，做笔记时，学生自己决定哪些信息重要并进行学习，这正是独立学习的核心特征。这种自主决策和学习的过程直接对应了"independent learning"的概念。

items.title: taking presentation notes
items.analysis: 文中Annie明确表示她认为做演讲笔记是一场"灾难"（disaster），并解释了原因：教授说得太快，而她写得太慢。这些描述都明确表明她觉得做演讲笔记很困难，因此正确答案是E选项"find it difficult"。
